build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player recruits gladiators from a market by spending gold. Each gladiator has combat stats (strength, speed, defense), a weapon preference, and a crowd appeal rating. Recruited gladiators join the roster. Score 0 if gladiators cannot be recruited, if they have no stats, or if recruitment is free. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Gladiators are trained between fights by assigning training regimens. Training improves stats over time but costs gold. Different regimens improve different stats. Score 0 if training does not exist, if it has no stat effect, or if it is free. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Fight nights are scheduled and the player picks matchups. Fights play out automatically based on gladiator stats. The crowd reacts to dramatic moments. Winners earn prize gold; losers may be injured or killed. Score 0 if fights cannot be scheduled, if combat has no resolution, or if there are no consequences. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A gold economy drives the arena: revenue from ticket sales and sponsors, expenses from recruitment, training, upkeep, and medical bills. A gold counter is permanently visible. Score 0 if there is no gold system or if income and expenses are absent. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The full loop connects: title -> new season -> recruit/train/schedule -> fight night -> results -> next week. The game tracks arena prestige and season wins. A season end shows final results. Score 0 if any transition is missing or if there is no season structure. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least four gladiator types exist with different weapon styles (sword/shield, trident/net, dual blades, heavy mace) affecting combat behavior and crowd appeal. Score 0 if all gladiators fight identically or if fewer than three styles exist. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Training regimens (strength drills, sparring, showmanship) have different effects on different stats. The player must choose which stats to prioritize for each gladiator. Score 0 if only one training type exists or if all training is identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Opponents vary in difficulty and style. Visiting challengers and rival arena fighters have different stats and fighting approaches. Later season opponents are stronger. Score 0 if all opponents are identical or if difficulty does not escalate. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Arena prestige grows with exciting fights and famous gladiators, attracting bigger crowds and richer sponsors. Prestige is tracked and affects revenue. Score 0 if prestige does not exist or if it has no effect on income. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Betting allows the player to wager gold on fight outcomes for extra profit. Upsets can occur, making betting a genuine risk/reward decision. Score 0 if betting does not exist or if outcomes are predetermined. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The HUD shows gold, arena prestige, roster summary, and calendar/schedule in clear non-overlapping regions. During fights, gladiator HP and crowd excitement are visible. Score 0 if gold, prestige, or fight state is missing. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The arena compound view shows training grounds, barracks, and arena floor as distinct areas. During fights, gladiators and the crowd are visible. Management and combat screens are clearly different. Score 0 if areas are indistinguishable or if fights have no visual arena. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Actions produce visible feedback: recruiting shows gladiator join roster, training shows stat changes, fights show combat with hits and reactions, crowd excitement builds visibly, and injuries/deaths are shown. Score 0 if actions produce no observable change. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Overall art coherence: arena, gladiators, crowd, training grounds, and UI belong to a consistent Roman-fantasy visual language with sand-and-steel grandeur. Score 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Gladiator sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art warriors with distinct weapon styles and armor. Different types have unique silhouettes. Score 0 if gladiators are plain colored shapes. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

The arena floor uses real textured sand with blood stains and weapon marks. The surrounding colosseum has illustrated architecture with crowd seating. Score 0 if the arena is a flat colored area. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets with Roman chrome: gladiator cards have scroll/tablet styling, gold displays have coin motifs, and menus have stone-and-iron backgrounds. Score 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and atmosphere: gladiators fight with attack animations, crowd cheers and reacts, training shows movement, blood/sparks fly during combat, and the arena has ambient atmosphere (torch flicker, dust motes). Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
